content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum


To     :    Barbara A. Milman                              Date    :    July 23, 1985
                                                           No. M-85-163


From   :    FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
            Diane Maura Fishburn [illegible]

Subject:   Personal Loans to Legislators Made by Major Donor/Lobbyist
           Employer


FACTS:

     The majority shareholder of a close corporation has been a
major donor for two years.  Recently he/the corporation became a
lobbyist employer.  He has made personal loans to legislators
(interest only, callable at will) to be used for business and
real estate investments.  The money does not go to the
legislators' campaign committees.  As far as he knows, these
loans have been disclosed on the legislators' SEIs.

QUESTION

     How should these loans be reported?

ADVICE:

     The loans should be reported on the lobbyist employer report
as activity expenses if made during the period covered by the
statement.  The outstanding loans do not have to be reported on a
continuing basis.

COMMENT:

     We should consider this situation when we amend our lobying
regulations.

Senator James H. Mills
119 - 13th Street
Del Mar, CA  92014




Dan Stanford, Chairman
FPPC
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804




[SEAL]

Senator Wadie P. Deddeh
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA  95814

Dan Stanford, Chairman
FPPC
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804
